name: Dont'e Thornton
headline: Field-stretching deep threat who must fix catch-rate problems
body:
Thornton's 2025 tape reads like a one-dimensional vertical specialist: 16.6 air yards per target and a staggering 49.7 air yards per reception, yet only 10 grabs on 30 looks across 11 games. That 33.3% catch rate is alarming, and the production behind it was thin at 135 yards and zero touchdowns. A -0.401 EPA per play and 30% success rate confirm the targets sent his way actively hurt the offense, compounded by three drops and three picks thrown into his coverage.

The usage hints at a real role, 45.3% of snaps and 41.8% route participation, but a 6.1% target share and 0.20 WOPR cap any meaningful fantasy ceiling. At 22, the raw deep speed gives him a path, and if the hands and route nuance tighten, the air-yards profile could spike for big plays. As constructed, though, he's a boom-or-bust dart throw, not a startable weapon. Treat him as a late-round flier only.
